drafts.title: Shared vote prompt causes same-direction model drift, breaking multi-voter independence
drafts.compiledTruth: Three voters receiving the same vote prompt suffer same-direction systematic drift because the prompt itself injects bias across all models. Observed: gpt-5.5 and claude-opus-4-7 both flagged injectionSuspected=true because the vote prompt's own marker examples ('ignore previous') were treated as real injections. Multi-model voting only defends against per-model random drift, not shared-prompt systematic drift. Genuine independence requires either different prompts per voter or a non-prompt-based dedup mechanism like lookup tools.

drafts.title: Forcing LLMs to output strict JSON caused parse failure via three compounding modes
drafts.compiledTruth: Demanding strict JSON output from LLMs collapsed robustness through three compounding failure modes: (a) DeepSeek wrapped JSON in typescript code blocks; (b) DeepSeek prefixed output with a Chinese prose preamble; (c) Claude truncated JSON mid-string when context exceeded token budget. Root cause: forcing structural correctness deprives models of expressive freedom, and any tradeoff toward natural prose breaks JSON.parse.

drafts.title: SKIP verdicts dominate sediment cost when full context is re-reviewed every round
drafts.compiledTruth: Most agent_end rounds produce SKIP verdicts (nothing worth recording), yet each round burns ~1M tokens (100K-365K context x 3 voters x thinking=high) to re-review the full session history and confirm 'still nothing new.' The dominant cost is repeatedly verifying the absence of novelty, not the occasional write. An incremental-window plus checkpoint design eliminates this waste.

drafts.title: Absence of a checkpoint cursor causes unbounded context growth per round
drafts.compiledTruth: v6.5 reloaded the entire 1700+ entry session branch every round without trimming already-processed entries. No cursor tracked what had been examined, so context grew unbounded (up to 365K tokens per reload) even though most content was already reviewed and dismissed in prior rounds. A persistent lastProcessedEntryId cursor is necessary to bound the working window to new entries only.

drafts.title: Blind quorum deduplication writes duplicates because voters cannot see the knowledge base
drafts.compiledTruth: Three voters independently agree an entry is worth writing, but none can see the existing knowledge base. Result: multiple voters frequently agree to write entries that already exist. Deduplication should happen proactively via model-driven lookup (search + get tools querying the store) before the write decision, not as retrospective quorum reconciliation after blind voting.

drafts.title: Single agent with lookup tools beats multi-voter for sediment dedup and reliability
drafts.compiledTruth: Single agent with lookup tools (search + get querying existing knowledge) outperforms multi-voter on four axes: (1) real dedup via tool-based store queries vs fake independence from shared prompts; (2) markdown terminators yield 100% parse success vs forced JSON at 0%; (3) 1x model call on incremental window vs 3x calls on full context; (4) every tool call auditable vs quorum-voting black box. Single-agent does not pretend independence; it achieves it through actual data access.
drafts.sourceHeading: 为什么单 agent 比多 voter 强

drafts.title: Markdown terminators with regex extraction beat JSON schema for LLM output parsing
drafts.compiledTruth: Parse LLM output via regex-extracted header key-value pairs and a free markdown body delimited by a __CONTENT__ marker. No JSON escaping required: code blocks, quotes, emoji, and foreign languages pass through intact. The model stays in natural prose mode; parsing is deterministic regex, not a second LLM pass. This eliminates the structural-correctness-vs-content-quality tradeoff that makes JSON.parse brittle against real LLM output.
drafts.sourceHeading: 为什么 markdown 不 JSON

drafts.title: DeepSeek V4 Pro is faster, cheaper, and more decisive than Claude Opus for sediment evaluation
drafts.compiledTruth: On identical 1900-entry / 200K-char context: claude-opus-4-7 used 6 turns, 12 tool calls, 85s to write new; deepseek-v4-pro used 4 turns, 8 tool calls, 69s to SKIP (correctly deduped against existing page). DeepSeek is 5x cheaper ($0.43/M vs $5/M input), makes fewer and more decisive tool calls, and correctly identifies duplicates faster. reasoning=high is sufficient; xhigh is unnecessary for this evaluation task.
drafts.sourceHeading: 为什么默认 deepseek-v4-pro 不 claude

drafts.title: Sediment checkpoint advances only on terminal success; failures preserve cursor for retry
drafts.compiledTruth: The lastProcessedEntryId cursor advances only on terminal success (SKIP, SKIP_DUPLICATE, or write success). Parse failures, network errors, and write errors preserve the cursor so the same window retries next round. On compaction, when lastProcessedEntryId disappears from the new branch, degrade to head-only window (size=1) to avoid replaying the entire pre-compaction history.

drafts.title: Sediment config uses three-tier fallback: env vars, settings.json, hardcoded defaults
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment model/reasoning/timeout configuration resolves via three tiers: (1) environment variables (highest priority, for ad-hoc debugging); (2) settings.json under piStack.sediment.singleAgent (persistent config); (3) hardcoded defaults: deepseek-v4-pro, reasoning=high, 180s timeout, 200-char minWindow, 16K maxTokens. Current effective config is logged to the audit trail on every session_start for traceability.

drafts.title: Removing the schema-enforcer step eliminates a secondary LLM injection surface
drafts.compiledTruth: v6.6's key security improvement over v6.5: the schema-enforcer step that re-fed voter output into a second LLM pass is removed entirely. This eliminates a secondary injection surface where attacker-controlled content in voter output would be processed by another model call. Markdown output is now parsed directly by deterministic regex, with no second LLM in the pipeline. Pre-LLM secret redaction and post-LLM secret rescan are retained.

drafts.title: Hybrid single-voter plus second-voter for maxims rejected as unnecessary complexity
drafts.compiledTruth: A proposed hybrid (single voter for most entries, second voter only for maxim-type entries) was rejected. It adds complexity without benefit: maxim judgment does not need a second opinion when lookup tools already verify against the existing knowledge base. The full single-agent approach is both simpler and equally effective since dedup correctness comes from tool-based store queries, not from voting.
